page: OVHcloud r2-30 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel cores of the Haswell architecture, running at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. The instance is provisioned with 30.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of a bundled 50 GB SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the r2-30 is positioned at the lower end of the compute spectrum, reflecting its older Intel processor architecture. Single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show a similar profile, with large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10%, though small-block cached reads perform relatively better. Database operations, such as Redis SET, achieve 2.93 million operations per second, which is categorized as weak.

The primary tradeoff of the r2-30 is its modest processing power relative to its generous memory allocation. While it offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU throughput, it is poorly suited for compute-heavy tasks, software compilation, or real-time rendering. This resource profile makes the instance appropriate for lightweight caching layers, small in-memory databases, or staging environments where memory capacity is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: OVHcloud r2-30 is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs on an Intel Haswell architecture running at 2.2 GHz, paired with 30.0 GB of RAM and a 50 GB local SSD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per vCPU. Benchmark data indicates modest processing capabilities, with CPU and memory bandwidth performance generally falling into the lower percentiles. Consequently, the r2-30 represents a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not demand intensive CPU throughput. It is best suited for lightweight in-memory databases, caching layers, and staging environments where memory capacity is prioritized over raw computational speed.
og_description: OVHcloud r2-30 is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 30 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads with modest CPU requirements.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server offering a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight caching and memory-intensive workloads.
